118	if the existing `:rbi_bole` digest differs from `«DIGEST»` — a touchmark collision; the step fails loud rather than clobber the prior capture (banish the Lode first, or ensconce under a fresh touchmark). The guard is digest-aware, not naive fail-if-exists, so retries pass; it is co-located with the copy in this same step — atomic with the GAR write in that it admits no host pre-submit window
119	.. Copy the upstream image into the Lode package with `gcrane cp` (the whole reference — manifest list and all per-platform manifests — digest preserved byte-for-byte) under the `:rbi_sha256-«DIGEST»` tag
120	.. Apply the remaining member tags by `gcrane tag` (a registry-side re-tag of the copied manifest, no blob round-trip): `:rbi_bole` (uniform handle) and `:«SANITIZED-ORIGIN»-«SHA10»` (fingerprint)
121	.. Author the
122	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
123	envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-2`, all keys sprued `rblv_`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, single-element `rblv_members[]`) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}
124	.. Write the per-slot result JSON to `buildStepOutputs`
125	
126	//axhos_step
127	. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
128	.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package
129

90	... Copy the upstream image registry->registry with `gcrane --platform linux/amd64 cp` (daemonless — no docker pull/tag/push; the single amd64 manifest only) into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_<tool>` — a distinct manifest per tool, all sharing the one package; no digest or fingerprint layer
91	... Record the upstream tag's manifest-list digest with `gcrane digest` for the envelope — the same canonical value docker's `RepoDigests` reported, so recorded digests are unchanged from the pre-eviction docker path
92	... *Single-platform capture is conclave-only and load-bearing.* The cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on amd64 workers, so other platforms are dead bytes — and capturing the full multi-arch index would make the Lode package a parent-index / child-manifest web: GAR refuses to delete a child manifest while its parent index still exists (`FAILED_PRECONDITION`, "manifest is referenced by parent manifests"), so a single `packages delete` of such a web removes nothing and banish must delete by convergence over multiple rounds (the cloud-dispatched delete handles this — see RBSLB and RBSCB; also link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/repositories/cleanup-policy[GAR cleanup policy] — child images referenced by a parent manifest are not deletable until the parent is removed). bole and wsl keep full-fidelity capture. This `--platform` flag must not propagate to other Lode kinds.
93	.. Author the
94	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
95	envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-2`, all keys sprued `rblv_`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, `rblv_members[]` one element per tool — the cardinality axis) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}
96	.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`
97	
98	//axhos_step
99	. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
100	.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package
101

122	.. Fetch the published checksums + detached signature; receive the signing key by the pinned fingerprint into a clean keyring; verify the signature; extract the expected digest for the target filename
123	.. Fetch the rootfs tarball (`curl`) and verify its bytes against that GPG-verified published digest; fail loud on mismatch
124	.. Wrap the opaque tarball as a single-layer OCI member (`FROM scratch` + `COPY`) and push it into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_rootfs` — no digest or fingerprint layer
125	.. Author the
126	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
127	envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-2`, all keys sprued `rblv_`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, `rblv_members[]` one element — the cardinality axis — carrying `rblv_verification` `gpg-sha256-published` and the per-member `rblv_signing_key` fingerprint) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}
128	.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`
129	
130	//axhos_step
131	. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
132	.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package
133

160	.. Acquire an anonymous registry-v2 pull token for the public quay family (Www-Authenticate discovery)
161	.. Fetch the family index at the pinned version; fail loud if it is not a multi-arch index
162	.. For each curated `disktype:arch` whose member tag is NOT already in the present-set, select the child descriptor matching `platform.architecture` + `annotations.disktype` — failing loud, listing every available pair, when a curated entry matches nothing (so a quay rotation reads as an inventory diff); a present-set member is spliced into the envelope verbatim and never re-resolved upstream (add-only)
163	.. Fetch each newly-selected leaf manifest; assert exactly one disk-blob layer; record its digest and declared size
164	.. Author the
165	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
166	envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-2`, every key sprued `rblv_`, trust grade `recorded-at-acquisition`, `rblv_members[]` one element per leaf — the cardinality axis — each carrying `rblv_verification` `recorded`, the captured leaf digest, and the per-member `rblv_acquired_at` / `rblv_capture_build`) and stage it plus the selection list — the newly-captured leaves only — on the {rbtgr_workspace}
167	.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs` — sized against the channel's 4KB raw-byte cap (CBG CBh_103): the slot payload carries the full envelope, and a worst-case 8-leaf native-family payload measures 3211 bytes (351 bytes per member × 8, ~340 envelope header, ~60 slot wrapper), leaving 885 bytes of headroom — the envelope-in-slot shape holds to ~10 leaves, past which this arithmetic must be redone
168	
169	//axhos_step
170	. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl08, gcrane)*:
171	.. For each selection row, `gcrane cp «FAMILY»@«LEAF_DIGEST»` into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_<disktype>-<arch>` — by digest, get-or-error, daemonless
172	.. Confirm each copied tag resolves to the selected digest (a free manifest-level readback)
173	.. Each leaf is a single-platform manifest, so the package stays FLAT — no parent-index web — keeping banish single-call atomic by construction
174	
175	//axhos_step
176	. *Residency Step (in-pool — rbgjl09)*:
177	.. For each member, HEAD the GAR registry-v2 blob and assert its `Content-Length` equals the leaf manifest's declared layer size

file.content:
.. Acquire an anonymous registry-v2 pull token for the public quay family (Www-Authenticate discovery)
.. Fetch the family index at the pinned version; fail loud if it is not a multi-arch index
.. For each curated `disktype:arch` whose member tag is NOT already in the present-set, select the child descriptor matching `platform.architecture` + `annotations.disktype` — failing loud, listing every available pair, when a curated entry matches nothing (so a quay rotation reads as an inventory diff); a present-set member is spliced into the envelope verbatim and never re-resolved upstream (add-only)
.. Fetch each newly-selected leaf manifest; assert exactly one disk-blob layer; record its digest and declared size
.. Author the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-2`, every key sprued `rblv_`, trust grade `recorded-at-acquisition`, `rblv_members[]` one element per leaf — the cardinality axis — each carrying `rblv_verification` `recorded`, the captured leaf digest, and the per-member `rblv_acquired_at` / `rblv_capture_build`) and stage it plus the selection list — the newly-captured leaves only — on the {rbtgr_workspace}
.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs` — sized against the channel's 4KB raw-byte cap (CBG CBh_103): the slot payload carries the full envelope, and a worst-case 8-leaf native-family payload measures 3211 bytes (351 bytes per member × 8, ~340 envelope header, ~60 slot wrapper), leaving 885 bytes of headroom — the envelope-in-slot shape holds to ~10 leaves, past which this arithmetic must be redone

//axhos_step
. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl08, gcrane)*:
.. For each selection row, `gcrane cp «FAMILY»@«LEAF_DIGEST»` into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_<disktype>-<arch>` — by digest, get-or-error, daemonless
.. Confirm each copied tag resolves to the selected digest (a free manifest-level readback)
.. Each leaf is a single-platform manifest, so the package stays FLAT — no parent-index web — keeping banish single-call atomic by construction

//axhos_step
. *Residency Step (in-pool — rbgjl09)*:
.. For each member, HEAD the GAR registry-v2 blob and assert its `Content-Length` equals the leaf manifest's declared layer size
.. The anti-hollow-mirror guard the recorded grade demands — a registry can hold a manifest whose large layer never fully landed, which the digest readback alone would not catch

//axhos_step
. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package
.. The recipe order is load-bearing: the vouch push runs strictly after the residency guard, so the provenance artifact never precedes the integrity check

//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_lode_underpin}

NOTE: This operation captures a vendor-published WSL rootfs into a single
{rbtga_lode}
— one
{gar_service}
package under the
{gar_lodes_namespace}
namespace, identified by a host-minted
{rbtga_touchmark}.
It is the wsl kind: a project-controlled Linux rootfs tarball, captured as the
substrate seed a Windows workload's `wsl --import` consumes.
It is the structural outlier among the Lode kinds — its upstream is an HTTPS
tarball plus an out-of-band published checksum, NOT an OCI registry image — so it
reuses neither the gcrane (bole) nor the docker (reliquary) registry-pull steps.

NOTE: Intent is declarative — no FQIN, no pinned digest.
Underpin takes the substrate version as two arguments (release + point, e.g.
`24.04 4`); the
{rbtr_director}
's workstation assembles the resolved tarball URL from a path-convention template,
and the cloud step DISCOVERS the checksum at capture time.
No full URL and no digest live in project config — per the no-FQIN principle, the
declaration stays intent and the pipeline computes the coordinate.

NOTE: Acquisition runs cloud-side.
The
{rbtr_director}
submits a
{gcb_service}
job on the tethered (egress-permitted) pool, executed by the
{rbtr_mason}
service account via the metadata server.
The workstation assembles the URL but never fetches the rootfs bytes — and never
performs the signature check, which is security-critical and so belongs in the
trusted GCP environment, not on a potentially-compromised station.

NOTE: Underpin verifies against the vendor's published, GPG-signed checksum.
The cloud step fetches the published `SHA256SUMS` and its detached signature,
fetches the signing key BY a pinned fingerprint into a clean keyring (so the
keyserver is not trusted — only a signature from exactly that key passes),
verifies the signature, extracts the expected digest for the target filename, then
verifies the fetched rootfs bytes against it.
This is the `verified-against-published` trust grade in its strongest form: the
vendor publishes and signs the checksum; RB pins only the signing key.

NOTE: Underpin bootstraps from no reliquary.
Its acquisition tool is curl over HTTPS (with gnupg and docker buildx, all present
on the Google-hosted `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` builder), not a registry pull,
so it needs no reliquary tool cohort.
The opaque tarball is wrapped as a single-layer OCI member by `FROM scratch` +
`COPY` (never `ADD`, which would extract and destroy the opaque blob).

NOTE: *Dirty-tree guard*.
Underpin refuses on a working tree with unstaged or staged changes, per the uniform Lode capture guard — see {rbtgo_lode_ensconce}.

NOTE: Underpin is capture-pure.
It mints the Lode, emits the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
provenance envelope, and reports the touchmark — but writes NO consumer config.
Consumption (a host's `wsl --import` of the captured seed, and the nameplate
election that pins which touchmark a node provisions) is a separate, deferred
consumption-side layer that reads the
{rbf_fact_lode}
this operation writes; it is not part of underpin.

The
{rbtr_director}
captures a vendor rootfs into a Lode:

//axhos_step
. *Authenticate as
{rbtr_director}*:
.. Execute
{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
.. {rbbc_store}
`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls

//axhos_step
. *Assemble the resolved URL (host-side)*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«URL»` by formatting the version arguments (release, release.point, arch) into
the cdimage path-convention template — the only resolution the workstation does
.. The template, the default architecture, and the pinned signing-key fingerprint
are RB constants; the version is the operator's (later, a nameplate's) declarative choice

//axhos_step
. *Mint Touchmark (host-side)*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«TOUCHMARK»` as the wsl kind-letter `w` + `YYMMDDHHMMSS` from current time
.. The host owns the stamp so the touchmark — and thus the
{rbf_fact_lode}
path — is known before the build
.. Example: `w260608120000`

//axhos_step
. *Submit Two-Step Capture Cloud Build*:
.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
... Step 1 — the Google-hosted docker builder image, script `rbgjl04-underpin-capture.sh` (the curl + GPG-verify + opaque-blob-wrap capture step)
... Step 2 — the Google-hosted docker builder image, script `rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (the vouch push)
... substitutions carrying GAR host/path, the
{gar_lodes_namespace}
root, `«TOUCHMARK»`, the rootfs member tag, the acquiring SA email, the trust grade, the envelope schema, the assembled `«URL»`, and the pinned signing-key fingerprint
... serviceAccount:
{rbtr_mason}
... pool: tethered (upstream fetch requires public egress)
.. {rbbc_submit}
link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/create[builds.create] with:
... project:
{rbtgi_depot_project}
... location:
{rbrd_gcp_region}
.. {rbbc_store}
`«BUILD_ID»` from response `.metadata.build.id`
.. {rbbc_show}
build dispatched with `«BUILD_ID»` and the Cloud Console URL

//axhos_step
. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl04)*:
.. Derive the sibling `SHA256SUMS` / `SHA256SUMS.gpg` URLs and the target filename from `«URL»`
.. Fetch the published checksums + detached signature; receive the signing key by the pinned fingerprint into a clean keyring; verify the signature; extract the expected digest for the target filename
.. Fetch the rootfs tarball (`curl`) and verify its bytes against that GPG-verified published digest; fail loud on mismatch
.. Wrap the opaque tarball as a single-layer OCI member (`FROM scratch` + `COPY`) and push it into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_rootfs` — no digest or fingerprint layer
.. Author the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-2`, all keys sprued `rblv_`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, `rblv_members[]` one element — the cardinality axis — carrying `rblv_verification` `gpg-sha256-published` and the per-member `rblv_signing_key` fingerprint) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}
.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`

//axhos_step
. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package

//axhos_step
. *Wait for Build Completion*:
.. {rbbc_poll}
link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/get[cloudbuild.builds.get] for `«BUILD_ID»`
.. {rbbc_require}
final status is `SUCCESS`

//axhoot_typed_output
{rbf_fact_lode}
. *Write Capture-Facts*:
.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` (base64-decoded JSON) from the completed build to recover the host-minted `«TOUCHMARK»`
.. {rbbc_store}
the touchmark value and the wsl kind-brand enum as the two bare single-form facts of the
{rbf_fact_lode}
— carrying no provenance content; the envelope is the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag, written cloud-side by the vouch step

//axhoc_completion
Successful execution captures the vendor rootfs into a single
{gar_service}
package at `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`, carrying the opaque rootfs blob
under its `:rbi_rootfs` member tag plus the `:rbi_vouch`
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope, and hands the touchmark forward host-side as the
{rbf_fact_lode}.
+
The operation is capture-pure: no vessel or nameplate config is written.
The captured seed is a Director-dictated version closure — the substrate bytes are
exactly the published, signature-verified rootfs, never "whatever the operator had
installed".
+
Consumption is deferred this heat: underpin stops at the registry.
A host's `wsl --import` of the captured seed, and the nameplate election that pins
which touchmark a node provisions, are a separate consumption-side layer that
reads the capture-file — so election retries independently and survives, and a
one-to-many fan-out across nodes iterates by reading the file rather than by a verb
taking N node arguments.

lines: .. Wrap the opaque tarball as a single-layer OCI member (`FROM scratch` + `COPY`) and push it into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_rootfs` — no digest or fingerprint layer
.. Author the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}

-envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-2`, all keys sprued `rblv_`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, `rblv_members[]` one element — the cardinality axis — carrying `rblv_verification` `gpg-sha256-published` and the per-member `rblv_signing_key` fingerprint) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}

+envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-3`, all keys sprued `rblv_`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, `rblv_members[]` one element — the cardinality axis — carrying `rblv_verification` `gpg-sha256-published` and the per-member `rblv_signing_key` fingerprint) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}
.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`
//axhos_step

The workstation assembles the URL but never fetches the rootfs bytes — and never
performs the signature check, which is security-critical and so belongs in the
trusted GCP environment, not on a potentially-compromised station.

NOTE: Underpin verifies against the vendor's published, GPG-signed checksum.
The cloud step fetches the published `SHA256SUMS` and its detached signature,
fetches the signing key BY a pinned fingerprint into a clean keyring (so the
keyserver is not trusted — only a signature from exactly that key passes),
verifies the signature, extracts the expected digest for the target filename, then
verifies the fetched rootfs bytes against it.
This is the `verified-against-published` trust grade in its strongest form: the
vendor publishes and signs the checksum; RB pins only the signing key.

NOTE: Underpin bootstraps from no reliquary.
Its acquisition tool is curl over HTTPS (with gnupg and docker buildx, all present
on the Google-hosted `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` builder), not a registry pull,
so it needs no reliquary tool cohort.
The opaque tarball is wrapped as a single-layer OCI member by `FROM scratch` +
`COPY` (never `ADD`, which would extract and destroy the opaque blob).

NOTE: *Dirty-tree guard*.
Underpin refuses on a working tree with unstaged or staged changes, per the uniform Lode capture guard — see {rbtgo_lode_ensconce}.

NOTE: Underpin is capture-pure.
It mints the Lode, emits the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
provenance envelope, and reports the touchmark — but writes NO consumer config.
Consumption (a host's `wsl --import` of the captured seed, and the nameplate
election that pins which touchmark a node provisions) is a separate, deferred
consumption-side layer that reads the
{rbf_fact_lode}
this operation writes; it is not part of underpin.

The
{rbtr_director}
captures a vendor rootfs into a Lode:

//axhos_step
. *Authenticate as
{rbtr_director}*:
.. Execute
{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
.. {rbbc_store}
`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls

//axhos_step
. *Assemble the resolved URL (host-side)*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«URL»` by formatting the version arguments (release, release.point, arch) into
the cdimage path-convention template — the only resolution the workstation does
.. The template, the default architecture, and the pinned signing-key fingerprint
are RB constants; the version is the operator's (later, a nameplate's) declarative choice

//axhos_step
. *Mint Touchmark (host-side)*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«TOUCHMARK»` as the wsl kind-letter `w` + `YYMMDDHHMMSS` from current time
.. The host owns the stamp so the touchmark — and thus the
{rbf_fact_lode}
path — is known before the build
.. Example: `w260608120000`

//axhos_step
. *Submit Two-Step Capture Cloud Build*:
.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
... Step 1 — the Google-hosted docker builder image, script `rbgjl04-underpin-capture.sh` (the curl + GPG-verify + opaque-blob-wrap capture step)
... Step 2 — the Google-hosted docker builder image, script `rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (the vouch push)
... substitutions carrying GAR host/path, the
{gar_lodes_namespace}
root, `«TOUCHMARK»`, the rootfs member tag, the acquiring SA email, the trust grade, the envelope schema, the assembled `«URL»`, and the pinned signing-key fingerprint
... serviceAccount:
{rbtr_mason}
... pool: tethered (upstream fetch requires public egress)
.. {rbbc_submit}
link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/create[builds.create] with:
... project:
{rbtgi_depot_project}
... location:
{rbrd_gcp_region}
.. {rbbc_store}
`«BUILD_ID»` from response `.metadata.build.id`
.. {rbbc_show}
build dispatched with `«BUILD_ID»` and the Cloud Console URL

//axhos_step
. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl04)*:
.. Derive the sibling `SHA256SUMS` / `SHA256SUMS.gpg` URLs and the target filename from `«URL»`
.. Fetch the published checksums + detached signature; receive the signing key by the pinned fingerprint into a clean keyring; verify the signature; extract the expected digest for the target filename
.. Fetch the rootfs tarball (`curl`) and verify its bytes against that GPG-verified published digest; fail loud on mismatch
.. Wrap the opaque tarball as a single-layer OCI member (`FROM scratch` + `COPY`) and push it into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_rootfs` — no digest or fingerprint layer
.. Author the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-3`, all keys sprued `rblv_`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, `rblv_members[]` one element — the cardinality axis — carrying `rblv_verification` `gpg-sha256-published` and the per-member `rblv_signing_key` fingerprint) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}
.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`

//axhos_step
. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package

//axhos_step
. *Wait for Build Completion*:
.. {rbbc_poll}
link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/get[cloudbuild.builds.get] for `«BUILD_ID»`
.. {rbbc_require}
final status is `SUCCESS`

//axhoot_typed_output
{rbf_fact_lode}
. *Write Capture-Facts*:
.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` (base64-decoded JSON) from the completed build to recover the host-minted `«TOUCHMARK»`
.. {rbbc_store}
the touchmark value and the wsl kind-brand enum as the two bare single-form facts of the
{rbf_fact_lode}
— carrying no provenance content; the envelope is the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag, written cloud-side by the vouch step

//axhoc_completion
Successful execution captures the vendor rootfs into a single
{gar_service}
package at `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`, carrying the opaque rootfs blob
under its `:rbi_rootfs` member tag plus the `:rbi_vouch`
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
envelope, and hands the touchmark forward host-side as the
{rbf_fact_lode}.
+
The operation is capture-pure: no vessel or nameplate config is written.
The captured seed is a Director-dictated version closure — the substrate bytes are
exactly the published, signature-verified rootfs, never "whatever the operator had
installed".
+
Consumption is deferred this heat: underpin stops at the registry.
A host's `wsl --import` of the captured seed, and the nameplate election that pins
which touchmark a node provisions, are a separate consumption-side layer that
reads the capture-file — so election retries independently and survives, and a
one-to-many fan-out across nodes iterates by reading the file rather than by a verb
taking N node arguments.

input.description: Find BCG command-substitution rules

content.content:
523:# From file using builtin $(<file) - NOT command substitution, a bash builtin
535:# Temp files INSTEAD of command substitution holds intermediate results
566:These rules govern **command substitution** — `$(command)`, which spawns a subshell, runs a command, and can fail. They do not apply to `$(<file)`, which despite sharing the `$()` syntax is a bash **builtin file redirect**: no subshell, no command, nothing to fail. `$(<file)` needs no `|| buc_die` guard — it takes content validation (`test -n` on the result) instead, since a successful read of an empty or missing file still yields the empty string.
569:# ❌ Anti-pattern: nested command substitution
572:# ❌ Anti-pattern: unguarded command substitution
792:#### Capture Functions (controlled command substitution)
796:Capture functions encapsulate command substitution to ensure predictable error handling and single-point-of-return semantics.
820:- Values requiring command substitution or pipeline processing
1049:- **_capture**: Need a value that requires command substitution or clean error handling
1151:# Never use test with command substitution that might expand to empty
1184:- **`$()` command substitution** — inside `_capture`/`_recite` functions only, or bash introspection (`compgen`, `declare -F`, `mktemp`); see **Capture Functions** and **Command Substitution Rules** checklist
1745:- [ ] `$(<file)` is a builtin redirect, not command substitution — needs content validation (`test -n`), NOT a `|| buc_die` guard

content.content:
#### ✅ CBh_102: The substitution blob is opaque to the spine; coverage is validated at dispatch

A per-kind body builds the substitution blob (`jq -n` → `_RBGx_` keys) and hands
it to the spine, which passes it through without reading any individual key
(keeping the spine kind-agnostic). Before the expensive submit, the validator
scans each include-expanded step body for the `_RBGx_` tokens it references
(*requires*) and fails if any is absent from the blob's keys (*provides*) — the
heat's one declared behavior-add, whose reject path carries its own unit tests.
A body author's obligation: every `_RBGx_` the body reads must be a key the
kind's blob provides.

*Cited by:* the validator's reject message; its unit tests.

#### ✅ CBh_103: A step returns data to the host via `buildStepOutputs`

`/workspace` does not survive the build. A step that must hand a value back writes
it to the `buildStepOutputs` channel (`/builder/outputs/output`); the spine
extracts the slot and base64-decodes it host-side into a capture file. This is
the only step→host return path. The slot is addressed **by step index**
(`.results.buildStepOutputs[N]`) — N is the producing step's position in the
recipe (the API contract orders outputs "corresponding to build step indices"),
so reordering recipe rows silently shifts slots; the body's extract call pins N
with a comment naming the producing step id. The channel is contract-capped:
**only the first 4KB is stored** (Build API, `results.buildStepOutputs`) — a
value that can outgrow 4KB does not belong on this channel.

*Cited by:* the spine's extract logic; any step producing a host-consumed value.

---

## Snippet & Exemplar Reference

Worked forms live where the code lives — read the current family members rather
than a curated filename list that drifts:

so reordering recipe rows silently shifts slots; the body's extract call pins N
with a comment naming the producing step id. The channel is contract-capped:
**only the first 4KB is stored** (Build API, `results.buildStepOutputs`) — a
value that can outgrow 4KB does not belong on this channel.

*Cited by:* the spine's extract logic; any step producing a host-consumed value.

---

Worked forms live where the code lives — read the current family members rather
than a curated filename list that drifts:

The body composes the substitutions object (the `_RBGL_*` keys) into a file; the
spine slots it into the Build resource's `substitutions` field and reads no key
from it. The spine adds exactly one key of its own — `_RBGL_GIT_COMMIT`, the
dispatching repo's HEAD commit — dispatch provenance it owns the same way it
owns the pool and timeout; the shared vouch-push step splices it into every
envelope as `rblv_git_commit`, and recipes without that step carry the key
unread. `options.automapSubstitutions` maps the keys into each step's
environment. The spine's opacity to the body's keys is what keeps it
registration-agnostic — a new kind authors a different blob without touching the
spine.

391	#### ✅ CBh_102: The substitution blob is opaque to the spine; coverage is validated at dispatch
392	
393	A per-kind body builds the substitution blob (`jq -n` → `_RBGx_` keys) and hands
394	it to the spine, which passes it through without reading any individual key
395	(keeping the spine kind-agnostic). Before the expensive submit, the validator
396	scans each include-expanded step body for the `_RBGx_` tokens it references
397	(*requires*) and fails if any is absent from the blob's keys (*provides*) — the
398	heat's one declared behavior-add, whose reject path carries its own unit tests.
399	A body author's obligation: every `_RBGx_` the body reads must be a key the
400	kind's blob provides.
401	
402	*Cited by:* the validator's reject message; its unit tests.

file.content:
#### ✅ CBh_102: The substitution blob is opaque to the spine; coverage is validated at dispatch

A per-kind body builds the substitution blob (`jq -n` → `_RBGx_` keys) and hands
it to the spine, which passes it through without reading any individual key
(keeping the spine kind-agnostic). Before the expensive submit, the validator
scans each include-expanded step body for the `_RBGx_` tokens it references
(*requires*) and fails if any is absent from the blob's keys (*provides*) — the
heat's one declared behavior-add, whose reject path carries its own unit tests.
A body author's obligation: every `_RBGx_` the body reads must be a key the
kind's blob provides.

*Cited by:* the validator's reject message; its unit tests.

it to the spine, which passes it through without reading any individual key
(keeping the spine kind-agnostic). The spine adds exactly one key of its own —
`_RBGL_GIT_COMMIT`, the dispatching HEAD commit, dispatch provenance it owns
like the pool and timeout; bodies never declare it. Before the expensive submit,
the validator scans each include-expanded step body for the `_RBGx_` tokens it
references (*requires*) and fails if any is absent from the blob's keys
(*provides*) — the heat's one declared behavior-add, whose reject path carries
its own unit tests. A body author's obligation: every `_RBGx_` the body reads
must be a key the kind's blob provides (or the spine's one injected key).

**₢BHAAq complete — the proof is exact.** The decoded envelope pulled from live GAR carries `Git commit: bdf07cdff3155041a3ac6f806b8d38d91eb87710` — which is precisely the commit that landed this pace's own code. The capture build dispatched from that HEAD, the spine injected it, rbgjl02 spliced it, GAR stored it, augur decoded it, and the fixture asserted the literal hash.

**Done-when, item by item:**
- Decoded envelope carries the dispatching commit — ✓ (trace `04-augur.txt` above)
- Schema bumped (`rbld-vouch-2` → `rbld-vouch-3`) and the field-list spec agrees — ✓ (RBS0 plus the five RBSL verb specs, present tense, no tidemark)
- `fast` green — ✓ 153/153
- Service-tier lode-lifecycle run proves end-to-end against live GAR — ✓ (and lode-collision passed in the same run)

**Shape as landed (all four cinches honored):** one new substitution `_RBGL_GIT_COMMIT`, injected by the spine into every blob (`rblds_Spine.sh`, reusing the existing `zrbfc_ensure_git_metadata` primitive — no new git plumbing); one splice at the shared vouch-push step `rbgjl02` so every kind inherits the field; additive field only.

**On your BCG/RCG compliance check** — I audited mid-stream and made two findings:
- One fix: my Rust used fully-qualified `std::process::Command` while the file already imports `Command` — corrected to the bare name. The rest matched (the match-on-output idiom mirrors `rbtdre_tree_clean`; bash uses the blessed `$(<file)` + `test -n` pattern, `zjq_` arg prefixes, `buc_die` guards; the cloud step uses `$(cat …)` since busybox sh lacks `$(<…)`, with CBi_101 presence asserts and idempotent splice).
- One contract consequence: CBG **CBh_102** and RBSCJ both declared the substitutions blob passes through the spine *verbatim* — my change makes the spine add exactly one key, so both contract texts were amended to record it (spine-owned dispatch provenance, like the pool and timeout).

Everything is committed (`bdf07cdff`); the tree is clean. Ready to wrap ₢BHAAq?
